question: An oreo shop sells $5$ different flavors of oreos and $3$ different flavors of milk. Alpha and Beta decide to purhcase some oreos. Since Alpha is picky, he will not order more than 1 of the same flavor. To be just as weird, Beta will only order oreos, but she will be willing to have repeats of flavors. How many ways could they have left the store with 3 products collectively? (A possible purchase is Alpha purchases 1 box of uh-oh oreos and 1 gallon of whole milk while Beta purchases 1 bag of strawberry milkshake oreos).

User: An oreo shop sells $5$ different flavors of oreos and $3$ different flavors of milk. Alpha and Beta decide to purhcase some oreos. Since Alpha is picky, he will not order more than 1 of the same flavor. To be just as weird, Beta will only order oreos, but she will be willing to have repeats of flavors. How many ways could they have left the store with 3 products collectively? (A possible purchase is Alpha purchases 1 box of uh-oh oreos and 1 gallon of whole milk while Beta purchases 1 bag of strawberry milkshake oreos).
Assistant: <think>
response: Since Alpha will not order more than 1 of the same flavor, we can model this as a combination problem. Alpha can either buy 3 different flavors, 2 different flavors + 1 repeat, or 1 flavor repeated 3 times. The number of ways Alpha can buy 3 different flavors is \( \binom{8}{3} \). The number of ways Alpha can buy 2 different flavors + 1 repeat is \( \binom{8}{2} \times 5 \) (since she can choose 1 of 3 milk flavors and repeat one of the 8 oreo flavors). The number of ways Alpha can buy 1 flavor repeated 3 times is \( \binom{8}{1} \). Therefore, the total number of ways Alpha can make his purchase is \( \binom{8}{3} + \binom{8}{2} \times 3 + \binom{8}{1} = 56 + 168 + 8 = 232 \). Beta will buy only oreos with repeats, so she can choose 1, 2, or 3 oreos from the 5 available flavors. The number of ways Beta can buy 1 oreo is \( \binom{5}{1} \). The number of ways Beta can buy 2 oreos is \( \binom{5}{2} \times 2 \) (since she can choose from the 2 flavors for repetition). The number of ways Beta can buy 3 oreos is \( \binom{5}{3} \). Therefore, the total number of ways Beta can make her purchase is \( \binom{5}{1} + \binom{5}{2} \times 2 + \binom{5}{3} = 5 + 20 + 10 = 35 \). The total number of ways they could leave the store with 3 products collectively is \( 232 \times 35 = 8120 \). </think> <answer> 8120 </answer>
